ops.title: P5 live finding: onward shortlist window crowd-out + silent optional-variant starvation (R-L, R-M)
ops.body:
Third P5 capstone live finding — the R-I crowd-out disease on a third axis, plus a disclosure gap. A4 driver (positioning plan: cash SFO→LAX optional, onward award →NRT; window 09-06..09-16, live): the onward LAX shortlist kept ONLY out-of-window soft-date padding rows (08-30/08-31/09-01, the cheapest per-cabin cohort) while the sweep held 27 in-window LAX→NRT J rows (+60 W, 135 Y). Zero in-window LAX candidates reached expand, so no positioned chain could pair with the 09-06+ cash positioning arrivals. The doomed padding-date chains expanded (quota spent) then all dropped at post-expansion continuity via a bare `continue`; expand.json showed 4 direct journeys and no trace of the positioned variant starving.

**R-L — window membership joins the cohort interleave.** `_cohort_select` gained window membership (in-window vs padded/out-of-window) as the OUTERMOST interleave axis, wrapping the R-I cabin interleave: within each window side cabins interleave as before, then the two window sides interleave above them. When both sides exist each gets rank-wise slots — padding near-misses stay represented (they exist to keep near-misses visible) AND in-window rows survive the budget. Diversity, not preference: no window-preference weighting inside selection. A pool entirely on one window side selects byte-identically to the cabin-only interleave (single-sided no-op, same as R-I's single-cabin no-op). Home-origin floor semantics unchanged. Window source: the leg's own window via `_window_dates(trip, leg)` (leg["window"] or trip["window"]) — read straight off the trip/leg intent, no sweep-provenance parse and no preference lookup; documented in the `_cohort_select` docstring. `_cohort_select` gained one `window_dates: set[str]` param, threaded from its single caller `shortlist()`; no premise contradiction (clean single-caller thread).

**R-M — variants are never silent.** When a plan carries optional legs, expand.json provenance gains `provenance["variants"]`: per variant (keyed by included leg ids joined with `+`), honest counts `chains_built`, `chains_expanded`, `dropped_continuity`, `journeys`. Added ONLY when the plan has optional legs (`any(leg.get("optional") ...)`) — no-optional plans keep byte-identical provenance (degeneracy pin). Disclosure only, no composition change: `_compose_chains` tracks counts and returns them; only the main (non-manual) compose call's stats attach. finalize does not render it this round (artifact is the record; board rendering can follow later). A positioned variant whose chains all die at continuity now reads `{journeys: 0, dropped_continuity: N}` while the direct variant shows its journeys.

Pins (each verified non-vacuously: reinject, watch the exact pin fail, restore): A4 mixed-window pool (unit + end-to-end) — reinject collapsing the window axis starved in-window rows (0==3); single-sided byte-identical; R-M variant accounting — reinject dropping the continuity counter failed dropped_continuity 0!=1. Full gate green: pytest 1124, ruff, ty, node 51.

Sibling to note 7dd34177 (R-I, cabin axis). Meta-lesson holds: selection-diversity changes need a mixed-axis pool fixture per axis — origin (P1 floor), cabin (R-I), now window (R-L).</body>
